{
  "risk": "MEDIUM",
  "riskReason": "Removed copy constructor in OAuth2GrantType.Context may cause state loss if code depended on cloning, and potential null pointer in TokenManager when encoder provider is unavailable.",
  "circularTests": {
    "detected": false,

  "findings": [
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "logic",
      "file": "server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/grants/OAuth2GrantType.java",
      "line": 102,
      "title": "Removed copy constructor breaks cloning pattern",
      "description": "The copy constructor `Context(Context context)` was removed from OAuth2GrantType.Context. If any code calls this constructor to clone a Context object, it will fail with a compilation error. The removal eliminates the ability to create independent copies of the context, which may be required for concurrent processing or isolation in OAuth2 grant flows.",
      "suggestion": "Retain the copy constructor or clearly document its removal and update any call sites. If removal is intentional, verify no code depends on Context cloning."
    },
    {
      "severity": "high",
      "category": "reliability",
      "file": "services/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/TokenManager.java",
      "line": 1051,
      "title": "Missing null check on TokenContextEncoderProvider",
      "description": "At line 1051, `session.getProvider(TokenContextEncoderProvider.class)` may return null if the provider is not available or not registered, but the code immediately calls `encoder.getTokenContextFromClientSessionContext()` without null checking. This will cause a NullPointerException at runtime if the provider is missing.",
      "suggestion": "Add a null check: `if (encoder == null) { throw new ProviderNotFoundException(...); }` or handle the absence gracefully before calling methods on the encoder."
    },
    {
      "severity": "medium",
      "category": "data-flow",
      "file": "server-spi-private/src/main/java/org/keycloak/protocol/oidc/grants/OAuth2GrantType.java",
      "line": 100,
      "title": "grantType initialized from formParams only in constructor",
      "description": "The `grantType` field is only set once during Context construction from `formParams.getFirst(OAuth2Constants.GRANT_TYPE)`. If formParams is later modified via `setFormParams()` method (line 105), the grantType field will not be updated, causing a stale value. This could lead to incorrect grant type tracking if formParams changes after construction.",
      "suggestion": "Either make grantType a derived property that reads from formParams dynamically, or update grantType in the setFormParams() method when formParams changes."
    }
  ]
}
